description:
India Book (IB) II, 66. Aden, August 1199. Sender and recipient are unknown, as only the lower part of this letter has been identified. The letter makes reference to the forced conversion of Yemenite Jews, and is connected to the affair mentioned in II, 67a (PGPID 5459). It also contains a discussion of Indian commodities; trade seems to have continued during this period of political and religious turmoil.

The letter relates the forced conversion of the Jews of Aden during the reign of al-Malik al-Muʿizz Ismāʿīl, self-styled caliph and nephew of Saladin. The letter's sender writes that the Jews of the Yemeni highlands had already apostatized, but most recently, Maḍmūn (identified with Maḍmūn b. David, head of the Jewish community of Yemen) had accepted Islam.

(Information from Goitein and Friedman, India Traders, 506–512).
Description from PGPID 867:
Letter of Madmun b. David, Aden, to Yiṣḥaq b. Sassun, Cairo, about the coercion of Aden jews to convert to Islam. Aden 1198.
description_he: ספר הודו II,66: מכתב על גירוש היהודים בעדן, המרת דת בכפייה, ועסקי הודו, אוגוסט 1199
